texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Abstract
texts.text: Abstract

texts.label: text
texts.orig: An  investigation  has been made  to  find  out whether membrane gas  separations  involve temperature changes. A thermodynamic study shows  that  these  separations  can be regarded as  isenthalpic processes. Dependent on the thermodynamic properties  of the gases,  the pressure  difference across  the membrane and  the stage  cut,  there appears to  be some  change  in temperature.
texts.text: An  investigation  has been made  to  find  out whether membrane gas  separations  involve temperature changes. A thermodynamic study shows  that  these  separations  can be regarded as  isenthalpic processes. Dependent on the thermodynamic properties  of the gases,  the pressure  difference across  the membrane and  the stage  cut,  there appears to  be some  change  in temperature.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This paper proposes a  computerized  method  for  calculating these  temperature changes.  Three examples of  gas separations are elaborated.  In two  cases,  an  air  separation and  a synthesis  gas separation,  the temperature modifications of the gas streams are found  to be less  than 5  'C. In another case,  a bio-gas separation,  there appears to  be a temperature decrease  of  more  than 30 'C in the  reject and  I5  'C in the  permeate.
texts.text: This paper proposes a  computerized  method  for  calculating these  temperature changes.  Three examples of  gas separations are elaborated.  In two  cases,  an  air  separation and  a synthesis  gas separation,  the temperature modifications of the gas streams are found  to be less  than 5  'C. In another case,  a bio-gas separation,  there appears to  be a temperature decrease  of  more  than 30 'C in the  reject and  I5  'C in the  permeate.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A simplified method  is presented  for  estimating  the  order  of  magnitude  of  the  temperature changes.  The results of  this  method  can be used for  deciding whether further  investigations on this  phenomenon are  required.
texts.text: A simplified method  is presented  for  estimating  the  order  of  magnitude  of  the  temperature changes.  The results of  this  method  can be used for  deciding whether further  investigations on this  phenomenon are  required.

texts.text: Untersucht  wurde, ob die  Trennung von  Gasen  iiber  Membranen mit TemperaturHnderungen einhergeht.  Eine thermodynamische Studie  zeigt, da13  diese  Trennungen als  isenthalpe  Vorglinge betrachtet werden  kdnnen.  Temperaturanderungen treten  in Abhgngigkeit  von  den  thermodynamischen Eigenschaften der Gase, dem  Unterschied  des Drucks  auf beiden Seiten  der Membran und  dem  Anteil  des  durchgetretenen Gases an der insgesamt zugefiihrten  Gasmenge (stage  cut)  ein.

In zwei FBllen,  und  zwar bei  einer  Lufttrennung  und  einer Synthesegastrennung, betrlgt  die  Temperaturanderung der  Gasstriime weniger als  5  'C.  Bei  einer  Biogaslrennung hingegen  ergab sich  ein Temperaturfall von  iiber  30 'C  beim  zuriickgebliebenen und  von  15  'C  im  durchgetretenen  Gas.
texts.text: Dieser Artikel  beschreibt  eine Methode fiir die  Berechnung dieser  Temperaturinderungen iiber  EDV. Drei Beispiele  fi,ir Gastrennungen wurden  ausgearbeitet. In zwei FBllen,  und  zwar bei  einer  Lufttrennung  und  einer Synthesegastrennung, betrlgt  die  Temperaturanderung der  Gasstriime weniger als  5  'C.  Bei  einer  Biogaslrennung hingegen  ergab sich  ein Temperaturfall von  iiber  30 'C  beim  zuriickgebliebenen und  von  15  'C  im  durchgetretenen  Gas.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Introduction
texts.text: Introduction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Membrane gas separations have  been  applied in industry  for  several years. The large  number of  recent papers shows  that  a lot  of  research  is  still being carried  out on this  subject.
texts.text: Membrane gas separations have  been  applied in industry  for  several years. The large  number of  recent papers shows  that  a lot  of  research  is  still being carried  out on this  subject.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: So far  no attention  has  been paid  to  the  occurrence of temperature  changes  involved in these  processes. There may  be an inclination  to  assume  that  the  pro- cess  conditions  are  isothermal. This assumption  is  not always valid,  as  will  be demonstrated  on the  basis  of a thermodynamic study in  this  paper.
texts.text: So far  no attention  has  been paid  to  the  occurrence of temperature  changes  involved in these  processes. There may  be an inclination  to  assume  that  the  pro- cess  conditions  are  isothermal. This assumption  is  not always valid,  as  will  be demonstrated  on the  basis  of a thermodynamic study in  this  paper.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In designing a membrane separation process  it  is very  important to  predict possible  temperature effects, because both the  separate  gas permeabilities and  the separation  factors are  temperature dependent. An  increasing  temperature causes the  permeabilities to  increase, whereas in  general  the  separation  factors tend to  decrease.
texts.text: In designing a membrane separation process  it  is very  important to  predict possible  temperature effects, because both the  separate  gas permeabilities and  the separation  factors are  temperature dependent. An  increasing  temperature causes the  permeabilities to  increase, whereas in  general  the  separation  factors tend to  decrease.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Membrane separations  can  be  regarded as adiabatic (Q  = 0) and there  is no  work done on the  system (I& = 0).  In this  case the sum of the enthalpies  of the outgoing  streams  is  equal to the feed enthalpy.  Consequently,  membrane gas  separations are  isenthalpic processes.  Under these  conditions eqn.  (6) can be derived  from  eqns. (  I), (3) and  (5):
texts.text: Membrane separations  can  be  regarded as adiabatic (Q  = 0) and there  is no  work done on the  system (I& = 0).  In this  case the sum of the enthalpies  of the outgoing  streams  is  equal to the feed enthalpy.  Consequently,  membrane gas  separations are  isenthalpic processes.  Under these  conditions eqn.  (6) can be derived  from  eqns. (  I), (3) and  (5):

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If  all  the streams  contain  perfect gases, the enthalpies of these streams  will  not  alter  as a result  of pressure changes or heat of mixing,  because  there are no  interactions  between the  gas  particles. In  that  case  the enthalpies of the gases are dependent on  the temperature only. Because the cnthalpies  do  not  change, the temperatures will  also remain constant.
texts.text: If  all  the streams  contain  perfect gases, the enthalpies of these streams  will  not  alter  as a result  of pressure changes or heat of mixing,  because  there are no  interactions  between the  gas  particles. In  that  case  the enthalpies of the gases are dependent on  the temperature only. Because the cnthalpies  do  not  change, the temperatures will  also remain constant.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Usually,  it  is  incorrect to assume that all  gases are perfect, especially in the case of high  feed pressures. For  imperfect  gases  the enthalpies  are dependent on pressure,  composition and temperature. Because of changes in pressure  and composition, eqn.  (6) can only  remain valid if  the temperatures of the streams change.
texts.text: Usually,  it  is  incorrect to assume that all  gases are perfect, especially in the case of high  feed pressures. For  imperfect  gases  the enthalpies  are dependent on pressure,  composition and temperature. Because of changes in pressure  and composition, eqn.  (6) can only  remain valid if  the temperatures of the streams change.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: By computerizing  the  described  step-by-step  method, the  temperature course of  the  membrane gas separator  can be calculated  relatively easily.
texts.text: By computerizing  the  described  step-by-step  method, the  temperature course of  the  membrane gas separator  can be calculated  relatively easily.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: From this  study it  can  be  seen that no  detailed knowledge of the membrane transport mechanisms (for  example, pore diffusion  or solution  diffusion)  is needed to  calculate temperature changes. It  is also  not important  to know whether  any  frictional forces  occur during permeation. The  temperature changes are determined  by the thermodynamic properties  of the involved gas streams only.
texts.text: From this  study it  can  be  seen that no  detailed knowledge of the membrane transport mechanisms (for  example, pore diffusion  or solution  diffusion)  is needed to  calculate temperature changes. It  is also  not important  to know whether  any  frictional forces  occur during permeation. The  temperature changes are determined  by the thermodynamic properties  of the involved gas streams only.

texts.orig: Calculation  examples
texts.text: Calculation  examples

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For the following binary separation examples  the temperature  courses have  been  calculated:  the removal  of  CO,  from bio-gas  or  landfill gas;  separation of  synthesis  gas;  air  separation.
texts.text: For the following binary separation examples  the temperature  courses have  been  calculated:  the removal  of  CO,  from bio-gas  or  landfill gas;  separation of  synthesis  gas;  air  separation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Gas  enthalpy values  are  calculated  with the  aid  of the  Redlich-Kwong equation of  state [ 11. The separation factors  are assumed to be independent  of the temperature. Furthermore,  the assumption is  made that there is  no  pressure drop  in the high pressure stream ( pF = pR).
texts.text: Gas  enthalpy values  are  calculated  with the  aid  of the  Redlich-Kwong equation of  state [ 11. The separation factors  are assumed to be independent  of the temperature. Furthermore,  the assumption is  made that there is  no  pressure drop  in the high pressure stream ( pF = pR).

The  permeate  pressure  is  2  bar.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Separation  of synthesis gas
texts.text: Separation  of synthesis gas

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A  synthesis  gas stream of 100  bar and  40 'C from a  methane reformer  consists of  25 mol% carbon monoxide and  75 mol% hydrogen.  The  composition of  this  mixture needs to  be altered  before application in an oxo-process, which  requires  a 50150 mixture  of CO  and  H,. To  this end, a fraction  of  the  hydrogen  is removed by means  of  membrane separation.  The  H,enriched permeate  can be applied in  a hydrogenation process.
texts.text: A  synthesis  gas stream of 100  bar and  40 'C from a  methane reformer  consists of  25 mol% carbon monoxide and  75 mol% hydrogen.  The  composition of  this  mixture needs to  be altered  before application in an oxo-process, which  requires  a 50150 mixture  of CO  and  H,. To  this end, a fraction  of  the  hydrogen  is removed by means  of  membrane separation.  The  H,enriched permeate  can be applied in  a hydrogenation process.

texts.orig: The  bio-gas example shows  that major  temperature  changes  may occur. In designing such a process this  should  be taken into consideration.  The  process may be  strongly  influenced  as  a  result of  these changes, for example a doubled membrane surface

texts.orig: The  other examples  appear  to give rise to only slight temperature changes. The  magnitude as  well  as the  direction  of  these  changes  are  connected  with the magnitude  of  the  Joule-Thomson coefficient, which is defined as
texts.text: The  other examples  appear  to give rise to only slight temperature changes. The  magnitude as  well  as the  direction  of  these  changes  are  connected  with the magnitude  of  the  Joule-Thomson coefficient, which is defined as

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  extent  of  the  temperature change  during an isenthalpic  gas expansion  is  determined  by p.  The  magnitude of  p depends  on component properties, pressure and  temperature. Table  1  shows  some  values of  p at 25 'C and  10  bar.
texts.text: The  extent  of  the  temperature change  during an isenthalpic  gas expansion  is  determined  by p.  The  magnitude of  p depends  on component properties, pressure and  temperature. Table  1  shows  some  values of  p at 25 'C and  10  bar.

texts.label: caption
texts.orig: TABLE I.  Joule-Thomson coefficients of  some pure  gases  at 10  bar  and  25 'C, calculated with the aid of the Redlich-Kwong equation of state
texts.text: TABLE I.  Joule-Thomson coefficients of  some pure  gases  at 10  bar  and  25 'C, calculated with the aid of the Redlich-Kwong equation of state

texts.label: text
texts.orig: From the  Table it  follows  that  CO,  and  CH,  both have large  values of p,  which  explains  the large  temperature change.  Furthermore, it  shows  that pHz is negative,  explaining a temperature  increase  involved with  isenthalpic  expansion of hydrogen-rich  gases, such as in the  synthesis  gas case.
texts.text: From the  Table it  follows  that  CO,  and  CH,  both have large  values of p,  which  explains  the large  temperature change.  Furthermore, it  shows  that pHz is negative,  explaining a temperature  increase  involved with  isenthalpic  expansion of hydrogen-rich  gases, such as in the  synthesis  gas case.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  Table  also shows  that  in the case of separations with  NH,or  H,S-containing streams  large temperature changes  are to  be expected.
texts.text: The  Table  also shows  that  in the case of separations with  NH,or  H,S-containing streams  large temperature changes  are to  be expected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The magnitude of  the  temperature change will  also depend  on the stage  cut 8 and  the  pressure  difference Ap  across the membrane. The  larger  8 or Ap,  the greater  the  expected temperature  change.
texts.text: The magnitude of  the  temperature change will  also depend  on the stage  cut 8 and  the  pressure  difference Ap  across the membrane. The  larger  8 or Ap,  the greater  the  expected temperature  change.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Simple method for  estimating the  order  of magnitude of  the  temperature changes
texts.text: Simple method for  estimating the  order  of magnitude of  the  temperature changes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It  would  be  practical if there  were a  way  to  roughly estimate the temperature  change.  By  making some assumptions  a simple method  can be found.
texts.text: It  would  be  practical if there  were a  way  to  roughly estimate the temperature  change.  By  making some assumptions  a simple method  can be found.

texts.orig: Table  2 shows  the calculated and  the estimated temperatures of the outgoing  gas streams from  the calculation  examples. The estimates  appear to  give  an impression of the  order of magnitude and  the  direction  of  the  temperature change. In this way  it is possible to predict  whether a severe  temperature modification may  occur and further  investigations are required.
texts.text: Table  2 shows  the calculated and  the estimated temperatures of the outgoing  gas streams from  the calculation  examples. The estimates  appear to  give  an impression of the  order of magnitude and  the  direction  of  the  temperature change. In this way  it is possible to predict  whether a severe  temperature modification may  occur and further  investigations are required.
